items.title: Going beyond the archives to revive a street's lost history

children.content: It's been a three year labour of love probing into the past and building on the fragments of a hidden history to write 'Logan St' and get it to the stage.

gallery.altText: Alexis West and Ali Ammouchi sit on stage in rehearsals for Logan St.

thumbnail.altText: Alexis West and Ali Ammouchi sit on stage in rehearsals for Logan St.

items.title: Special guest dazzles aspiring astronauts at Space Day Out

children.content: Young South Australians got one step closer to achieving their space exploration dreams on Sunday when they met a "real-life" astronaut.

image.altText: A lady in a blue astronaut suit looks at the camera with a smile

items.title: 'Almost certainly new' species of giant earthworms found in SA

children.content: A South Australian ecologist has been tracking giant earthworm sightings for 30 years, despite the species not officially existing in the state.

image.altText: A child holding up a worm which stretches from above his head to his waist

items.title: New energy scheme adding to bills, SA government admits

children.content: A new SA government energy scheme set to collect almost $44 million from electricity users this financial year has been described by a critic as a "hidden charge".

image.altText: A Stobie pole with powerlines.

items.title: From 40 cartographers to two, street directories are nearing end of the road

children.content: While sales of the once-ubiquitous street directories have fallen dramatically since their peak in the 1980s, they still have a niche market.

image.altText: A street directory sits on a steering wheel

items.title: More than 20 SA Department for Child Protection employees stood down

children.content: At the end of February, South Australia's Department for Child Protection had 12 open investigations into staff as a result of a care concern, Freedom of Information documents show.

image.altText: Empty swings in a park with a pram parked in the background.

items.title: Rohan Dennis pleads guilty to driving while disqualified

children.content: Former professional cyclist and two-time Olympian Rohan Dennis pleads guilty after he was caught driving while disqualified, not far from where his wife died two-and-a-half years ago.

image.altText: Former Olympic cyclist Rohan Dennis outside the Adelaide Magistrates Court.

items.title: Three years after fatal shark attack, community cements teacher's legacy

children.content: A regional community was left shaken after Simon Baccanello was killed in a shark attack in 2023. Now, his legacy will be cemented through a new sporting complex.

gallery.altText: A smiling man wearing a light blue shirt with his arms down and hands together

thumbnail.altText: A smiling man wearing a light blue shirt with his arms down and hands together

items.title: Wok the Duck owners seething after compulsory acquisition shock

children.content: Aston Hills Mount Barker developers and a popular restaurant say their investments have been turned upside down by a state government compulsory acquisition announced this week.

image.altText: Three people stand in a restaurant with a sign behind them saying 'boom'

items.title: Nude shopper admits fourth indecent act in Mount Gambier

children.content: A Mount Gambier man who previously admitted going naked in two local food businesses pleads guilty to another charge of indecent behaviour, as his case heads to the new South Australian Veterans Court.

image.altText: A shop on a main street with a sign saying OK PIE SHOP

items.title: Cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers dies aged 89

children.content: Sir Don Bradman once described Sobers as "the greatest all-round cricketer I ever saw".

image.altText: Garry Sobers 2

items.title: Man caught driving homemade car fashioned from mobility scooter

children.content: The disqualified driver had made the car from a gopher mobility scooter and the body of a child's ride-on car, and will appear in court at a later date.

image.altText: A homemade car at a police station showing a very small child's car with modifications

items.title: Controversial 'blue blob' makes it to first birthday relatively unscathed

children.content: The blue sculpture that's been the subject of love, loathing, debate and international headlines for googly eyed-vandalism has made it to its first birthday relatively unscathed.

gallery.altText: A blue public art statue wearing a large party hat

thumbnail.altText: A blue public art statue wearing a large party hat

items.title: Retired teacher says year 12 marks are being inflated in 'unfair' grading system

children.content: A retired South Australian teacher says some educators are inflating the grades of year 12 students, which she says is making the marks "unfair".

image.altText: A teacher in front of a classroom, seen from behind.

items.title: ATO asked to justify 'extravagant' $73m Adelaide office move

children.content: The Australian Taxation Office will spend at least $73.4 million to relocate its offices into "the best building in Adelaide", four streets away.

image.altText: Two high-rise office building in a green open space

items.title: Federal MP and staffer injured in boating accident

children.content: Tasmanian police say a man fell from an oyster barge when it hit a sandbar and was briefly trapped. Member for Barker Tony Pasin says he and a staffer were injured and now receiving medical treatment, but it is not clear who fell from the barge.

image.altText: A man with a Liberal logo on his jacket standing in front of a garden

items.title: SA victims of alleged childcare centre abuser identified, AFP says

children.content: "A couple" of alleged victims from South Australia have been identified as part of an investigation into a Sydney childcare worker accused of abusing more than 130 children, federal police say.

image.altText: Cloise up of man grinning in a green shirt, plant behind him

items.title: Community meets over plans for large 'AI factory' at River Murray town

children.content: An AI company says its proposed projects have the potential to create hundreds of jobs in South Australia, but residents of one River Murray town say they want to know more about the potential impacts.

image.altText: Tailem Bend locals at a public forum.

items.title: Ambulances arriving late to lower-priority patients, SA opposition says

children.content: Data obtained by the South Australian opposition shows fewer ambulances are arriving on time to priority 3 and 4 patients.

image.altText: An ambulance driving on a city street with buildings on either side

items.title: Hopes bones in underwater 'time capsules' will unlock more prehistory

children.content: Scientists who have been finding fossils in SA's underwater caves are hoping they can give a window into environmental changes.

image.altText: An animal bone at the bottom of sinkhole with a diver in a black wetsuit and holding a torch.

items.title: Researchers develop laser technology to test drinks for methanol

children.content: Adelaide University researchers have developed a way to use lasers to test closed alcohol bottles for methanol, which kills hundreds of people every year.

image.altText: A man with glasses wearing a grey jumper poses next to a glass bottle with brown fluid in it

items.title: Sara Albrecht took on Trump's tariffs. She got 'violent' calls

children.content: When a law centre decided to fight Donald Trump's "liberation day" tariffs, it was acutely aware of possible "retaliation" from a president who takes matters "personally".

image.altText: A woman stands in front of multiple microphones with two men in suits standing behind her.

items.title: Boys who embrace 'expansive masculinity' likely to fare better

children.content: These men are being the role models they never had. They hope to curb Australia's masculinity crisis.

gallery.altText: a young boy smiles at the camera, while hugging his dad whose face we can't see

thumbnail.altText: a young boy smiles at the camera, while hugging his dad whose face we can't see

items.title: As Europe follows Australia's social media ban, the US opts for own way

children.content: More and more countries are following Australia's lead and introducing social media bans for children, but US policies are piecemeal and vary from state to state.

image.altText: Alexis sits at an outdoor table with a metal fence and trees in the background.

items.title: 'Lack of evidence' means Lao officials won't lay charges over teens' deaths

children.content: Investigators in Laos gave one of their first detailed accounts of the investigation into the deaths of six people, including 19-year-old Australians Holly Morton-Bowles and Bianca Jones, saying they don't have enough evidence to lay charges because autopsies were not carried out.

image.altText: Side-by-side photos of Bianca Jones and Holly Bowles, two Melbourne teens who have been hospitalised in Thailand

items.title: In a secret safe at this luxury house, police struck gold

children.content: Indonesian police investigating suspected corruption discover millions of dollars worth of currency and 74 kilograms of gold in a safe hidden in a luxury house near Jakarta.

gallery.altText: Gold bars on two suitcases pictured from above.

thumbnail.altText: Gold bars on two suitcases pictured from above.

items.title: Quiet change to Nauru policing powers could thwart legal challenges

children.content: Advocates question the timing of law changes that coincide with mounting federal court challenges from individuals fighting deportation.

image.altText: A Nauru man in a black police officers shirt, stands with his hands on a desk.

items.title: The Houdini of Boggo Road escaped the prison in 1946. He shouldn't have taken a friend

children.content: In 1946, Arthur 'Slim' Halliday recruited two associates, scaled the walls of Boggo Road Gaol for a second time, hopped in a taxi and vanished.

image.altText: A graphic collage of three prison escapees, a news article, and Boggo Road Gaol.

items.title: Australia's only full-time penny-farthing maker is breaking world records

children.content: Farmer-turned-professional penny-farthing maker Dan Bolwell has a cult following of international enthusiasts.

gallery.altText: Dan Bolwell in his workshop kneeling beside a penny farthing
A huge penny farthing and a tiny one, surrounded by supporters in London

thumbnail.altText: Dan Bolwell in his workshop kneeling beside a penny farthing
A huge penny farthing and a tiny one, surrounded by supporters in London
